children.text: Argument Overview
children.label: section_header

children.text: The Marrowfield equilibrium thesis holds that provincial guild coalitions stabilize only after a threshold number of rival factions independently arrive at the same distributive claim, rather than when any single faction musters enough strength to prevail outright.
children.label: text

children.text: This equilibrium account, rather than any simple resource-mobilization story, best explains why provincial guild petitions cluster around a narrow set of shared demands even when the underlying grievances animating each faction differ substantially.
children.label: text
